QUALITY ASSURANCE CASE STUDY (SANITIZED DEMO)

Project: Cross-Border Checkout Reliability
Role: Software QA Engineer
Period: Q1 2026

1) Context
A logistics platform release introduced multi-currency checkout, role-based approval, and callback-based payment confirmation.

2) Risk
- Currency mismatch between checkout UI and payment response
- Approval race condition when approver action and callback happen simultaneously
- Order status inconsistency under network retry

3) Approach
- Risk-based test charter for high-impact financial flows
- Structured data matrix (currency x role x region x order state)
- API negative testing for timeout, duplicate callback, malformed payload
- Pre-release triage by severity, user impact, and rollback complexity

4) Key Findings
- Rounding mismatch in two currency scenarios
- Approval-state race condition under concurrent events
- Inconsistent order status after callback retry

5) Impact
- Critical issues found before production
- Better release confidence for payment and approval flows
- Reduced support escalation risk for launch window

6) Redaction Note
Client names, IDs, endpoints, and personal data are masked.
